verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/BiP is canonically ER-luminal but undergoes stress-induced translocation to the cell surface. Under ER stress and in cancer cells, HSPA5 relocates to the plasma membrane where it exists as a peripheral protein, mostly anchored via non-covalent interactions with other cell-surface proteins and/or GPI-anchored proteins. This stress-induced (ER stress, hypoxia, chemotherapy, cancer cell states) surface pool qualifies as a documented, conditional accessibility route. The protein is not stably surface-resident at baseline but is accessible from the extracellular space under defined pathological contexts.
